summary:
Claim: Carbon contamination in phosphate-bonded investment leads to black castings due to chemical reactions during casting.  

Evidence: - Carbon reacts with molten iron-rich alloys (e.g., steel, gray cast iron) forming toxic gases (CO/CO₂). - These gases cause porosity and result in visible black oxide layers on the casting surface.  

Answer: B – Black castings
formatted_summary:

Evidence: - Carbon reacts with molten iron-rich alloys (e.g., steel, gray cast iron) forming toxic gases (CO/CO₂). - These gases cause porosity and result in visible black oxide layers on the casting surface.  

Answer: B – Black castings. Answer choice: B
